title: Help to debug a zero pull

posts.name: Jieun Yoo
posts.text:
Dear stats & combine experts,
Hello. I am looking for advice on what looks like a zero pull for some NPs.
Here is the directory:
https://gitlab.cern.ch/cms-analysis/hig/hig-24-008/datacards/-/tree/master/VH?ref_type=heads
These are the commands I ran:
combineCards.py SR1pass2016APV=SR1pass2016APV.txt SR1fail2016APV=SR1fail2016APV.txt  TopCRpass2016APV=TopCRpass2016APV.txt SR1pass2016=SR1pass2016.txt SR1fail2016=SR1fail2016.txt TopCRpass2016=TopCRpass2016.txt SR1pass2017=SR1pass2017.txt SR1fail2017=SR1fail2017.txt  TopCRpass2017=TopCRpass2017.txt SR1pass2018=SR1pass2018.txt SR1fail2018=SR1fail2018.txt  TopCRpass2018=TopCRpass2018.txt > model_combined.txt
text2workspace.py model_combined.txt -o workspace.root
combine -M MultiDimFit -n _initialFit_Test --algo singles --redefineSignalPOIs r --rMin -20 --rMax 20 --robustFit 1 -m 200 -d workspace.root --setParameters r=1 -t -1 --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 1
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d workspace.root -m 200 --rMin -20 --rMax 20 --robustFit 1 --doFits -t -1 --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 1
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d workspace.root -m 200 --rMin -20 --rMax 20 --robustFit 1 --output impacts.json -t -1
For an individual year, e.g.,
combineCards.py SR1pass2017=SR1pass2017.txt SR1fail2017=SR1fail2017.txt TopCRpass2017=TopCRpass2017.txt > model_combined.txt
there is no error message given at all when running with the above commands.
And, the same issue (zero pulls for impacts low in the ranking) happens with just one year as well as with multiple years. In the past, I tried cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0, but that led to a failure to find crossings. Also I tried robustHesse but that led to an error message about inability to compute pulls. I tried to change rMin and rMax as well but that didn’t seem to fix the issue
cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 1 seems to work without an error message printed to the screen, but gives the possibly? strange behavior about zero pulls.
From the datacard it seems there should be a lnN uncertainty on signal only, which should show up in the pulls. I attach a sample impact plot as well. Is the fit failing (silently?) for some reason? Or how do I interpret the zero pulls?
2017.pdf (36.9 KB)
Thanks for any suggestions.
Sincerely,
Jieun
posts.cooked_html:
Dear stats & combine experts,

Hello. I am looking for advice on what looks like a zero pull for some NPs.

Here is the directory:
https://gitlab.cern.ch/cms-analysis/hig/hig-24-008/datacards/-/tree/master/VH?ref_type=heads

These are the commands I ran:

combineCards.py SR1pass2016APV=SR1pass2016APV.txt SR1fail2016APV=SR1fail2016APV.txt TopCRpass2016APV=TopCRpass2016APV.txt SR1pass2016=SR1pass2016.txt SR1fail2016=SR1fail2016.txt TopCRpass2016=TopCRpass2016.txt SR1pass2017=SR1pass2017.txt SR1fail2017=SR1fail2017.txt TopCRpass2017=TopCRpass2017.txt SR1pass2018=SR1pass2018.txt SR1fail2018=SR1fail2018.txt TopCRpass2018=TopCRpass2018.txt > model_combined.txt

text2workspace.py model_combined.txt -o workspace.root

combine -M MultiDimFit -n _initialFit_Test --algo singles --redefineSignalPOIs r --rMin -20 --rMax 20 --robustFit 1 -m 200 -d workspace.root --setParameters r=1 -t -1 --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 1

combineTool.py -M Impacts -d workspace.root -m 200 --rMin -20 --rMax 20 --robustFit 1 --doFits -t -1 --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 1

combineTool.py -M Impacts -d workspace.root -m 200 --rMin -20 --rMax 20 --robustFit 1 --output impacts.json -t -1

For an individual year, e.g.,
combineCards.py SR1pass2017=SR1pass2017.txt SR1fail2017=SR1fail2017.txt TopCRpass2017=TopCRpass2017.txt > model_combined.txt

there is no error message given at all when running with the above commands.

And, the same issue (zero pulls for impacts low in the ranking) happens with just one year as well as with multiple years. In the past, I tried cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0, but that led to a failure to find crossings. Also I tried robustHesse but that led to an error message about inability to compute pulls. I tried to change rMin and rMax as well but that didn’t seem to fix the issue

cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 1 seems to work without an error message printed to the screen, but gives the possibly? strange behavior about zero pulls.

From the datacard it seems there should be a lnN uncertainty on signal only, which should show up in the pulls. I attach a sample impact plot as well. Is the fit failing (silently?) for some reason? Or how do I interpret the zero pulls?

2017.pdf (36.9 KB)

Thanks for any suggestions.

Sincerely,
Jieun

posts.name: Andrea Carlo Marini
posts.text:
Hi,
this is the Asimov dataset.
Though in some analysis where (constrained) nuisances are only on the signal this may also happen on the observed dataset.
-A
